verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein expressed exclusively in sperm. In resting (acrosome-intact) sperm it resides on the inner acrosomal membrane and is inaccessible from outside the cell. Upon the acrosome reaction (an activation-induced exocytotic event), IZUMO1 translocates to the outer plasma membrane of the equatorial segment where it binds its egg-surface receptor JUNO to mediate sperm-egg adhesion and fusion. Surface display requires both (a) restriction to the sperm germline/gamete lineage and (b) acrosome-reaction activation. The protein has its own TM anchor, but expression and surface exposure are confined to this narrow reproductive lineage under a specific activation state, placing it in contextual/tissue_restricted_surface.
